rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference visible ports and features from the AADL model.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly typed in scope.
Avoid unsafe Boolean or numeric encodings of event ports or opaque data types.
Ensure implementation-level contracts do not reference subcomponent paths not visible in type scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Guarantees in implementation scope should relate visible outputs to inputs
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared variables
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values without explicit model support
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
